company.name: Oculis Holding
company.subtitle: DIAMOND DME Ph3 topline Q2 2026 · Privosegtor BTD for ON · 3 first-in-class candidates

market.notes: First-in-class neuroprotective. ACUITY Ph2: +18 letters. No approved therapies. US ~30K/yr. Rare disease pricing.

us.patientsK.note: AAO/NANOS: ~15K US optic neuritis/yr (MS-associated dominant); acute demyelinating episode.

eu.patientsK.note: ECTRIMS: ~20K EU-5 ON/yr; IV methylprednisolone standard acute; remyelination agents in trials.

row.patientsK.note: Global ON ~150K/yr; MS prevalence-driven; ex-US/EU ~12K accessible in MS specialty centers.

us.wtpPct.note: ~45% -- acute IV steroid standard; novel remyelinating agents (e.g., Clene CNM-Au8) late-stage trials.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~30% -- EMA orphan/fast-track pathways; HTA will require OCT RNFL + visual acuity endpoints.

row.wtpPct.note: ~15% -- MS specialty care limited outside OECD; acute steroid universal.

us.priceK.note: Remyelinating agent modeled ~$50K acute course; one-time/short-course pricing.

penPct.note: ~20% peak -- acute window + MS workflow integration; background steroid still SOC.

us.reachPct.note: Privosegtor (OCS-05) acute optic neuritis; ~10K US ON/yr (50% MS-associated); Ph3 ACUITY 2026; no approved ON neuroprotectant; launch 2028 ~30% reach.

us.priceK.note: ~$38K per acute course projected; anchor to Ocrevus $68K/yr MS or Solu-Medrol generic baseline SOC.

us.wtpPct.note: ~45% -- acute ON neuroprotection unmet need; Medicare Part B infused; MS advocacy (NMSS) supports novel neuroprotectant.

eu.reachPct.note: ~18% EU5; EMA ON novel acute path; NICE acute neuroprotection HST possible.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~25% -- G-BA ON orphan possible; France HAS MS centers.

row.reachPct.note: ~10% ROW; Japan PMDA ON novel acute.

market.notes: 2nd cause of optic nerve blindness after glaucoma. No treatment. Combined ON+NAION US market >$7B.

us.patientsK.note: AAO/NANOS: ~6K US non-arteritic AION/yr incidence; acute onset optic nerve ischemia, typically >50yr.

eu.patientsK.note: ESAO: ~8K EU-5 NAION/yr incidence; no approved Rx, supportive care only (aspirin, BP control).

row.patientsK.note: Global NAION ~60K/yr; ex-US/EU ~10K accessible in specialty ophthalmology centers.

us.wtpPct.note: ~50% -- zero approved Rx; Quark QPI-1007 P3 failed; Stoke STK-002 P1 early; high unmet need.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~30% -- EMA orphan pathway; HTA will require visual acuity endpoint (ETDRS letters gain).

row.wtpPct.note: ~10% -- acute care specialty access limited outside US/EU-5/Japan.

us.priceK.note: NAION Rx modeled ~$40K acute course (single/short course IVT); premium for approved agent.

penPct.note: ~15% peak -- narrow 14-day acute window limits addressable; late presentation common.

us.reachPct.note: NAION (non-arteritic ischemic optic neuropathy) ~6K US/yr; no approved Rx; Privosegtor Ph2 acute neuroprotection; launch 2029 ~25% reach.

us.priceK.note: ~$35K per acute course; orphan designation expected given no approved Rx.

us.wtpPct.note: ~40% -- NAION orphan + ophthalmology high unmet; Medicare Part B acute inpatient or ER setting.

eu.reachPct.note: ~15% EU5 NAION; ultra-niche, EMA orphan possible.

market.notes: First topical DME treatment. Eye drop vs monthly anti-VEGF injections. 800+ pts enrolled. Topline Q2 2026. NDA Q4 2026 if positive.

us.patientsK.note: CDC DiRECT + NEI: ~750K US diabetic macular edema; anti-VEGF (Eylea/Vabysmo/Lucentis) standard IVT Rx.

eu.patientsK.note: EU DME ~1M; anti-VEGF + dexamethasone implant (Ozurdex); biosimilar ranibizumab eroding originator.

row.patientsK.note: Global DME ~21M (IDF diabetes-driven); ex-US/EU ~500K on IVT therapy in specialty retina centers.

us.wtpPct.note: ~70% -- Medicare Part B coverage; anti-VEGF workhorse; Vabysmo Q16W durability advantage vs Eylea Q8W.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~55% -- national health coverage; biosimilar ranibizumab cost pressure; Vabysmo HTA ongoing.

row.wtpPct.note: ~30% -- Japan/Korea strong anti-VEGF use; emerging markets bevacizumab off-label compounded.

us.priceK.note: Eylea HD ~$11K/yr (Q8-16W IVT); Vabysmo ~$12K/yr; Lucentis ~$14K/yr; biosimilars ~$5K/yr.

eu.priceK.note: Eylea ~$6K/yr post-HTA; biosimilar ranibizumab ~$2K/yr dominant in several markets.

row.priceK.note: ~$2K/yr blended; compounded bevacizumab <$0.2K/dose common in emerging markets.

penPct.note: ~35% peak of diagnosed DME on IVT therapy; suboptimal adherence + undertreatment limits ceiling.

us.reachPct.note: OCS-01 topical dexamethasone cyclodextrin DME; Ph3 DIAMOND 2025; ~750K US DME; displaces intravitreal Eylea/Iluvien with topical; launch 2027 ~15% reach.

us.priceK.note: ~$12K/yr topical DME; vs Eylea $17K/yr, Iluvien $8.9K implant; OCS-01 positioned as convenient topical alt.

us.wtpPct.note: ~60% -- Medicare Part D topical ophthalmic; Eylea $2.2K/injection benchmark; topical convenience huge value but payors cautious.

eu.reachPct.note: ~22% EU5 DME; NICE TA824 Eylea/Iluvien SOC; topical DME novel EU.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~35% -- G-BA + NICE topical DME access via retina clinics post-DIAMOND data.

row.reachPct.note: ~12% ROW; Japan NHI DME anti-VEGF entrenched; China NRDL Eylea 2021.

market.notes: First genotype-based DED program. Topical anti-TNFa. PREDICT-1 results Q4 2026.

us.patientsK.note: AAO/NEI: ~16M US dry eye disease diagnosed; ~4M moderate-severe Rx-treated; Restasis/Xiidra/Miebo/Tyrvaya.

eu.patientsK.note: EU dry eye ~30M; ~5M Rx-treated; Ikervis (ciclosporin) + generics dominate; newer agents EMA pending.

row.patientsK.note: Global dry eye ~300M; ~15M ex-US/EU Rx-treated; Japan rebamipide (Mucosta) widely used.

us.wtpPct.note: ~40% -- crowded class Restasis generic, Xiidra (Bausch), Miebo (Bausch 2023), Tyrvaya (Viatris nasal spray).

eu.wtpPct.note: ~30% -- Ikervis reimbursed; HTA strict on incremental benefit; generic ciclosporin pressure.

row.wtpPct.note: ~20% -- Japan rebamipide standard; emerging markets mostly artificial tears OTC.

us.priceK.note: Xiidra ~$7K/yr WAC; Miebo ~$6K/yr; Tyrvaya ~$7K/yr; Restasis generic <$1K/yr.

row.priceK.note: Blended ~$0.5K/yr; Japan rebamipide ~$0.3K, emerging markets OTC tears dominate.

penPct.note: ~15% peak for branded Rx within diagnosed pool; high OTC artificial tear use caps Rx share.

us.reachPct.note: Licaminlimab (OCS-02) anti-TNF-alpha topical dry eye; Ph2b 2024; ~16M US DED, crowded (Xiidra, Restasis, Miebo, Tyrvaya); launch 2028 ~8% reach.

us.priceK.note: ~$6.5K/yr topical; anchored to Xiidra/Restasis for payor parity; novel anti-TNF MoA supports positioning.

us.wtpPct.note: ~55% -- DED Rx commercial + Medicare Part D broad; Xiidra $6.7K/yr + Restasis $6K benchmark; anti-TNF topical novel MoA.

eu.reachPct.note: ~12% EU5 DED; NICE DED historically restrictive; EMA topical biologic novel.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~25% -- NICE + G-BA DED Rx access via ophthalmology; cyclosporine generic pressure.

row.reachPct.note: ~8% ROW; Japan NHI DED broad; China NRDL DED expanding.

events: ✗ DIAMOND misses — topical DME doesn't work

bear.narrative: DME disappoints + Privosegtor narrowly works in ON only
events: ✗ OCS-01 modest effect, not enough vs injections
✓ Privosegtor works but narrow margin in ON

base.narrative: DIAMOND positive + Privosegtor first-in-class + DED signal
events: ✓ OCS-01 DME works — NDA Q4 2026, approval 2027
~ Licaminlimab shows signal in genotype subset

bull.narrative: DME blockbuster + neuroprotection platform across neuro diseases
events: ✓ Privosegtor expanded into glaucoma, MS, GA

psychedelic_bull.narrative: Neuroprotection redefines neuro-ophthalmology — M&A at premium
events: ✓ Privosegtor proven across ON, NAION, MS, glaucoma

lastData.title: Privosegtor BTD + PIONEER program launched
points: ACUITY Phase 2: +18 ETDRS letters at 3mo (2x visual resolution)
FDA Breakthrough Therapy designation for ON (Jan 2026)
PIONEER-1 started Q4 2025, PIONEER-2 H1 2026, PIONEER-3 mid-2026
Retinal + optic nerve structure preserved (MRI confirmed)
Clean safety: headache + acne 10.5%, no SAEs, no discontinuations
DIAMOND Ph3 (OCS-01 DME): 800+ pts, topline Q2 2026
Stifel PT raised to $50, 10 analyst Buy ratings

nextCatalyst.title: DIAMOND Phase 3 topline — OCS-01 eye drop in DME
thresholds: Primary: BCVA change vs sham at Week 24
Key: can topical delivery reach retinal drug levels for DME?
THE near-term binary event — DME is largest commercial opportunity

risks: DIAMOND is binary — topical DME has very limited precedent
Privosegtor Phase 2 was small (n=19/arm) — translation risk
NAION has no proven drug precedent — uncertain regulatory path
DME competition: Eylea, Vabysmo, new anti-VEGF mechanisms
DED market crowded — genotype approach unproven at scale
Pre-revenue — valuation entirely on pipeline probability
Blue sky weight (10%) reflects that Privosegtor platform value is real but early

catalysts.title: PIONEER-2 first-line ON registrational trial start
catalysts._rationale: PIONEER-2 planned to follow in first half of 2026 as second registrational trial for optic neuritis

catalysts.title: PIONEER-3 NAION registrational trial start
catalysts._rationale: PIONEER-3 in NAION expected to begin in mid-2026

catalysts.title: PREDICT-1 Phase 2/3 topline data (Licaminlimab precision medicine DED)
catalysts._rationale: First genotype-based registrational trial PREDICT-1 initiated with topline results expected Q4 2026
